INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(type, timestamp_ms DESC);\n144\t\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_zone ON events(zone, timestamp_ms DESC);\n145\t\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_person ON events(person, timestamp_ms DESC);\n146\t\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_archive (\n147\t\t\tid          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n148\t\t\ttimestamp_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n149\t\t\ttype        TEXT    NOT NULL,\n150\t\t\tzone        TEXT,\n151\t\t\tperson      TEXT,\n152\t\t\tblob_id     INTEGER,\n153\t\t\tdetail_json TEXT,\n154\t\t\tseverity    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n155\t\t);\n156\t\tCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_fts USING fts5(\n157\t\t\ttype, zone, person, detail_json,\n158\t\t\tcontent='events', content_rowid='id'\n159\t\t);\n160\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON events BEGIN\n161\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n162\t\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n163\t\tEND;\n164\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON events BEGIN\n165\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n166\t\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n167\t\tEND;\n168\t\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON events BEGIN\n169\t\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n170\t\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n171\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n172\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n173\t\tEND;\n174\t\t`\n175\t\t_, err := db.Exec(schema)\n176\t\treturn err\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t// isValidEventType checks whether the event type string is a known type.\n180\tfunc isValidEventType(t string) bool {\n181\t\tswitch t {\n182\t\tcase \"detection\", \"zone_entry\", \"zone_exit\", \"portal_crossing\",\n183\t\t\t\"trigger_fired\", \"fall_alert\", \"FallDetected\", \"anomaly\", \"AnomalyDetected\", \"security_alert\",\n184\t\t\t\"node_online\", \"node_offline\", \"ota_update\", \"baseline_changed\",\n185\t\t\t\"system\", \"learning_milestone\", \"sleep_session_end\", \"ZoneTransition\",\n186\t\t\t\"presence_transition\", \"stationary_detected\", \"anomaly_learned\", \"sleep_session_start\":\n187\t\t\treturn true\n188\t\t}\n189\t\treturn false\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// RegisterRoutes registers events endpoints.\n193\t//\n194\t// GET /api/events — paginated event list with FTS5 search and keyset cursor pagination.\n195\t//\n196\t//\tQuery params: limit (default 50, max 500), before (timestamp_ms cursor),\n197\t//\tsince (ISO8601), until (ISO8601), type (single type filter),\n198\t//\ttypes (comma-separated type list for category filtering),\n199\t//\tzone, zone_id, person, person_id, q (FTS5 query), mode (expert|simple).\n200\t//\n201\t// GET /api/events/{id} — single event by ID.\n202\t//\n203\t// POST /api/events/{id}/feedback — submit feedback for an event.\n204\tfunc (e *EventsHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n205\t\tr.Get(\"/api/events\", e.listEvents)\n206\t\tr.Get(\"/api/events/{id}\", e.getEvent)\n207\t\tr.Post(\"/api/events/{id}/feedback\", e.postEventFeedback)\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\t// eventsResponse is the JSON response for GET /api/events.\n211\ttype eventsResponse struct {\n212\t\tEvents        []*Event `json:\"events\"`\n213\t\tCursor        string   `json:\"cursor,omitempty\"`\n214\t\tHasMore       bool     `json:\"has_more\"`\n215\t\tTotalFiltered int      `json:\"total_filtered\"`\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// prepareFTSQuery appends a trailing * for prefix matching if the query\n219\t// doesn't already end with a FTS5 operator character. This enables\n220\t// partial word matching (e.g., \"kit\" matches \"kitchen\").\n221\tfunc prepareFTSQuery(q string) string {\n222\t\tq = strings.TrimSpace(q)\n223\t\tif q == \"\" {\n224\t\t\treturn q\n225\t\t}\n226\t\t// If the query already ends with a FTS5 special character or operator, leave it alone.\n227\t\tlast := q[len(q)-1]\n228\t\tif last == '*' || last == '\"' || last == ')' {\n229\t\t\treturn q\n230\t\t}\n231\t\t// For simple terms (no operators), append * for prefix matching.\n232\t\t// If the query contains FTS5 operators (AND, OR, NOT, NEAR), append * to each\n233\t\t// simple token instead.\n234\t\tif strings.Contains(q, \" AND \") || strings.Contains(q, \" OR \") ||\n235\t\t\tstrings.Contains(q, \" NOT \") || strings.Contains(q, \" NEAR \") {\n236\t\t\t// Has operators — append * to each token that isn't an operator or quoted phrase.\n237\t\t\tparts := strings.Fields(q)\n238\t\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n239\t\t\t\tif p == \"AND\" || p == \"OR\" || p == \"NOT\" || p == \"NEAR\" {\n240\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n241\t\t\t\t}\n242\t\t\t\tif (strings.HasPrefix(p, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(p, `\"`)) || p == \"(\" || p == \")\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tparts[i] = p + \"*\"\n246\t\t\t}\n247\t\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \" \")\n248\t\t}\n249\t\t// Simple single-term query — just append * for prefix matching.\n250\t\treturn q + \"*\"\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\tfunc (e *EventsHandler) listEvents(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n254\t\t// Parse limit\n255\t\tlimit := eventsDefaultLimit\n256\t\tif s := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n257\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(s); err == nil && n > 0 {\n258\t\t\t\tlimit = n\n259\t\t\t}\n260\t\t}\n261\t\tif limit > eventsMaxLimit {\n262\t\t\tlimit = eventsMaxLimit\n263\t\t}\n264\t\n265\t\t// Parse before cursor (timestamp_ms as string)\n266\t\tvar beforeTS int64\n267\t\tif s := r.URL.Query().Get(\"before\"); s != \"\" {\n268\t\t\tbeforeTS, _ = strconv.ParseInt(s, 10, 64)\n269\t\t}\n270\t\n271\t\t// Parse filters\n272\t\tq := r.URL.Query().Get(\"q\")\n273\t\teventType := r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\")\n274\t\ttypesStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"types\") // comma-separated list of event types\n275\t\tzone := r.URL.Query().Get(\"zone\")\n276\t\tzoneID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"zone_id\")\n277\t\tif zoneID != \"\" {\n278\t\t\tzone = zoneID // zone_id takes precedence over zone\n279\t\t}\n280\t\tperson := r.URL.Query().Get(\"person\")\n281\t\tpersonID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"person_id\")\n282\t\tif personID != \"\" {\n283\t\t\tperson = personID // person_id takes precedence over person\n284\t\t}\n285\t\tafterStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"after\")\n286\t\tsinceStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"since\") // Alias for after\n287\t\tuntilStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"until\") // Upper bound timestamp\n288\t\tmode := r.URL.Query().Get(\"mode\")      // \"expert\" or \"simple\" (default: simple)\n289\t\n290\t\t// Parse and validate types list (comma-separated)\n291\t\tvar typesFilter []string\n292\t\tif typesStr != \"\" {\n293\t\t\tfor _, t := range strings.Split(typesStr, \",\") {\n294\t\t\t\tt = strings.TrimSpace(t)\n295\t\t\t\tif t == \"\" {\n296\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n297\t\t\t\t}\n298\t\t\t\tif !isValidEventType(t) {\n299\t\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid event type: \"+t)\n300\t\t\t\t\treturn\n301\t\t\t\t}\n302\t\t\t\ttypesFilter = append(typesFilter, t)\n303\t\t\t}\n304\t\t}\n305\t\n306\t\t// Validate event type\n307\t\tif eventType != \"\" && !isValidEventType(eventType) {\n308\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid event type\")\n309\t\t\treturn\n310\t\t}\n311\t\n312\t\t// Validate after/since timestamp (prefer since if both provided)\n313\t\tvar afterTS int64\n314\t\ttimeStr := afterStr\n315\t\tif sinceStr != \"\" \"modernc.org/sqlite\"\n\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/events\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\teventsDefaultLimit = 50\n\teventsMaxLimit     = 500\n)\n\n// EventsHandler manages the events timeline.\ntype EventsHandler struct {\n\tmu             sync.RWMutex\n\tdb             *sql.DB\n\thub            DashboardHub\n\townsDB         bool\n\tfeedbackHandler any // FeedbackHandler for POST /api/events/{id}/feedback\n}\n\n// DashboardHub is the interface for broadcasting to dashboard clients.\ntype DashboardHub interface {\n\tBroadcastEventFromDB(id int64, timestamp int64, eventType, zone, person string, blobID int, detailJSON, severity string)\n}\n\n// Event represents a timeline event.\ntype Event struct {\n\tID         int64     `json:\"id\"`\n\tTimestamp  int64     `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n\tType       string    `json:\"type\"`\n\tZone       string    `json:\"zone,omitempty\"`\n\tPerson     string    `json:\"person,omitempty\"`\n\tBlobID     int it.\nfunc (h *EventsHandler) LogEvent(eventType string, timestamp time.Time, zone, person string, blobID int, detailJSON string, severity string) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tts := timestamp.UnixNano() / 1e6\n\tif severity == \"\" {\n\t\tseverity = \"info\"\n\t}\n\n\tresult, err := h.db.Exec(`\n\t\tINSERT INTO events (timestamp_ms, type, zone, person, blob_id, detail_json, Broadcast to dashboard clients\n\tif h.hub != nil {\n\t\th.hub.BroadcastEventFromDB(id, ts, eventType, zone, person, blobID, detailJSON, severity)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// SetHub sets the for event feedback endpoints.\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) SetFeedbackHandler(handler any) {\n\te.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer e.mu.Unlock()\n\te.feedbackHandler = handler\n}\n\n// NewEventsHandler creates a new events handler backed by a SQLite file at dbPath.\n// It opens the database, creates the schema, and takes ownership of the connection.\n// Use Close() to release resources.\nfunc NewEventsHandler(dbPath string) (*EventsHandler, error) {\n\tdb, err := sql.Open(\"sqlite\", dbPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"open events db: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif err := createEventsSchema(db); err != nil {\n\t\tdb.Close()\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"init events schema: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Events handler initialized (own DB: %s)\", dbPath)\n\treturn &EventsHandler{db: db, ownsDB: true}, nil\n}\n\n// NewEventsHandlerFromDB creates a new events handler using an existing database connection.\n// The events table schema must already exist (created by migrations 001 and 011).\nfunc NewEventsHandlerFromDB(db *sql.DB) *EventsHandler {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Events handler initialized (shared DB)\")\n\treturn &EventsHandler{db: db}\n}\n\n// Close releases resources. If the handler owns the DB connection, it closes it.\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) Close() {\n\tif e.ownsDB {\n\t\te.db.Close()\n\t}\n}\n\n// Archive runs the archive job to move old events to the archive table.\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) Archive(_ interface{}) {\n\tevents.RunArchiveJob(e.db)\n}\n\n// createEventsSchema creates the events, events_archive, and FTS5 tables.\nfunc createEventsSchema(db *sql.DB) error {\n\tschema := `\n\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (\n\t\tid          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,\n\t\ttimestamp_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n\t\ttype        TEXT    NOT NULL,\n\t\tzone        TEXT,\n\t\tperson      TEXT,\n\t\tblob_id     INTEGER,\n\t\tdetail_json TEXT,\n\t\tseverity    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n\t);\n\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_time ON events(timestamp_ms DESC);\n\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(type, timestamp_ms DESC);\n\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_zone ON events(zone, timestamp_ms DESC);\n\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_person ON events(person, timestamp_ms DESC);\n\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_archive (\n\t\tid          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n\t\ttimestamp_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,\n\t\ttype        TEXT    NOT NULL,\n\t\tzone        TEXT,\n\t\tperson      TEXT,\n\t\tblob_id     INTEGER,\n\t\tdetail_json TEXT,\n\t\tseverity    TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'info'\n\t);\n\tCREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events_fts USING fts5(\n\t\ttype, zone, person, detail_json,\n\t\tcontent='events', content_rowid='id'\n\t);\n\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON events BEGIN\n\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n\t\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n\tEND;\n\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON events BEGIN\n\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n\tEND;\n\tCREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS events_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON events BEGIN\n\t\tINSERT INTO events_fts(events_fts, rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n\t\tVALUES ('delete', old.id, old.type, old.zone, old.person, old.detail_json);\n\tINSERT INTO events_fts(rowid, type, zone, person, detail_json)\n\tVALUES (new.id, new.type, new.zone, new.person, new.detail_json);\n\tEND;\n\t`\n\t_, err := db.Exec(schema)\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// isValidEventType checks whether the event type string is a known type.\nfunc isValidEventType(t string) bool {\n\tswitch t {\n\tcase \"detection\", \"zone_entry\", \"zone_exit\", \"portal_crossing\",\n\t\t\"trigger_fired\", \"fall_alert\", \"FallDetected\", \"anomaly\", \"AnomalyDetected\", \"security_alert\",\n\t\t\"node_online\", \"node_offline\", \"ota_update\", \"baseline_changed\",\n\t\t\"system\", \"learning_milestone\", \"sleep_session_end\", \"ZoneTransition\",\n\t\t\"presence_transition\", \"stationary_detected\", \"anomaly_learned\", \"sleep_session_start\":\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers events endpoints.\n//\n// GET /api/events — paginated event list with FTS5 search and keyset cursor pagination.\n//\n//\tQuery params: limit (default 50, max 500), before (timestamp_ms cursor),\n//\tsince (ISO8601), until (ISO8601), type (single type filter),\n//\ttypes (comma-separated type list for category filtering),\n//\tzone, zone_id, person, person_id, q (FTS5 query), mode (expert|simple).\n//\n// GET /api/events/{id} — single event by ID.\n//\n// POST /api/events/{id}/feedback — submit feedback for an event.\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\tr.Get(\"/api/events\", e.listEvents)\n\tr.Get(\"/api/events/{id}\", e.getEvent)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/events/{id}/feedback\", e.postEventFeedback)\n}\n\n// eventsResponse is the JSON response for GET /api/events.\ntype eventsResponse struct {\n\tEvents        []*Event `json:\"events\"`\n\tCursor        string   `json:\"cursor,omitempty\"`\n\tHasMore       bool     `json:\"has_more\"`\n\tTotalFiltered int      `json:\"total_filtered\"`\n}\n\n// prepareFTSQuery appends a trailing * for prefix matching if the query\n// doesn't already end with a FTS5 operator character. This enables\n// partial word matching (e.g., \"kit\" matches \"kitchen\").\nfunc prepareFTSQuery(q string) string {\n\tq = strings.TrimSpace(q)\n\tif q == \"\" {\n\t\treturn q\n\t}\n\t// If the query already ends with a FTS5 special character or operator, leave it alone.\n\tlast := q[len(q)-1]\n\tif last == '*' || last == '\"' || last == ')' {\n\t\treturn q\n\t}\n\t// For simple terms (no operators), append * for prefix matching.\n\t// If the query contains FTS5 operators (AND, OR, NOT, NEAR), append * to each\n\t// simple token instead.\n\tif strings.Contains(q, \" AND \") || strings.Contains(q, \" OR \") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(q, \" NOT \") || strings.Contains(q, \" NEAR \") {\n\t\t// Has operators — append * to each token that isn't an operator or quoted phrase.\n\t\tparts := strings.Fields(q)\n\t\tfor i, p := range parts {\n\t\t\tif p == \"AND\" || p == \"OR\" || p == \"NOT\" || p == \"NEAR\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif (strings.HasPrefix(p, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(p, `\"`)) || p == \"(\" || p == \")\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tparts[i] = p + \"*\"\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \" \")\n\t}\n\t// Simple single-term query — just append * for prefix matching.\n\treturn q + \"*\"\n}\n\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) listEvents(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// Parse limit\n\tlimit := eventsDefaultLimit\n\tif s := r.URL.Query().Get(\"limit\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tif n, err := strconv.Atoi(s); err == nil && n > 0 {\n\t\t\tlimit = n\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif limit > eventsMaxLimit {\n\t\tlimit = eventsMaxLimit\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse before cursor (timestamp_ms as string)\n\tvar beforeTS int64\n\tif s := r.URL.Query().Get(\"before\"); s != \"\" {\n\t\tbeforeTS, _ = strconv.ParseInt(s, 10, 64)\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse filters\n\tq := r.URL.Query().Get(\"q\")\n\teventType := r.URL.Query().Get(\"type\")\n\ttypesStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"types\") // comma-separated list of event types\n\tzone := r.URL.Query().Get(\"zone\")\n\tzoneID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"zone_id\")\n\tif zoneID != \"\" {\n\t\tzone = zoneID // zone_id takes precedence over zone\n\t}\n\tperson := r.URL.Query().Get(\"person\")\n\tpersonID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"person_id\")\n\tif personID != \"\" {\n\t\tperson = personID // person_id takes precedence over person\n\t}\n\tafterStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"after\")\n\tsinceStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"since\") // Alias for after\n\tuntilStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"until\") // Upper bound timestamp\n\tmode := r.URL.Query().Get(\"mode\")      // \"expert\" or \"simple\" (default: simple)\n\n\t// Parse and validate types list (comma-separated)\n\tvar typesFilter []string\n\tif typesStr != \"\" {\n\t\tfor _, t := range strings.Split(typesStr, \",\") {\n\t\t\tt = strings.TrimSpace(t)\n\t\t\tif t == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !isValidEventType(t) {\n\t\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid event type: \"+t)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttypesFilter = append(typesFilter, t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate event type\n\tif eventType != \"\" && !isValidEventType(eventType) {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid event type\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate after/since timestamp (prefer since if both provided)\n\tvar afterTS int64\n\ttimeStr := afterStr\n\tif sinceStr != \"\" {\n\t\ttimeStr = sinceStr\n\t}\n\tif timeStr != \"\" {\n\t\tt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, timeStr)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid since/after timestamp\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tafterTS = t.UnixNano() / 1e6\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate until timestamp (upper bound)\n\tvar untilTS int64\n\tif untilStr != \"\" {\n\t\tt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, untilStr)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid until timestamp\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tuntilTS = t.UnixNano() / 1e6\n\t}\n\n\t// Person-relevant event types for simple mode\n\t// Simple mode shows only person-relevant events: zone_entry, zone_exit, ZoneTransition, portal_crossing, fall_alert, FallDetected, anomaly, AnomalyDetected, security_alert, sleep_session_end\n\t// Simple mode hides: node_online, node_offline, ota_update, baseline_changed, system, learning_milestone, detection, presence_transition, stationary_detected\n\tsimpleModeTypes := map[string]bool{\n\t\t\"zone_entry\":        true,\n\t\t\"zone_exit\":         true,\n\t\t\"ZoneTransition\":    true,\n\t\t\"portal_crossing\":   true,\n\t\t\"fall_alert\":        true,\n\t\t\"FallDetected\":      true,\n\t\t\"anomaly\":           true,\n\t\t\"AnomalyDetected\":   true,\n\t\t\"security_alert\":    true,\n\t\t\"sleep_session_end\": true,\n\t}\n\t// Default to expert mode; switch to simple mode only when explicitly requested\n\tisSimpleMode := mode == \"simple\"\n\n\t// Prepare FTS5 query with prefix matching\n\tif q != \"\" {\n\t\tq = prepareFTSQuery(q)\n\t}\n\n\t// Determine query mode: FTS5 or regular\n\tuseFTS := q != \"\"\n\tp := \"\" // column prefix for FTS JOIN queries\n\tif useFTS {\n\t\tp = \"e.\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Build SELECT columns and FROM clause\n\tselectCols := p + \"id, \" + p + \"timestamp_ms, \" + p + \"type, \" + p + \"zone, \" +\n\t\tp + \"person, \" + p + \"blob_id, \" + p + \"detail_json, \" + p + \"severity\"\n\n\tvar fromTable, baseWhere string\n\tvar baseArgs []interface{}\n\n\tif useFTS {\n\t\tfromTable = \"events e JOIN events_fts ft ON e.id = ft.rowid\"\n\t\tbaseWhere = \"events_fts MATCH ?\"\n\t\tbaseArgs = []interface{}{q}\n\t} else {\n\t\tfromTable = \"events\"\n\t\tbaseWhere = \"1=1\"\n\t\tbaseArgs = []interface{}{}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build WHERE clause with filters\n\twhereSQL := baseWhere\n\twhereArgs := append([]interface{}{}, baseArgs...)\n\n\tif eventType != \"\" {\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"type = ?\"\n\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, eventType)\n\t} else if len(typesFilter) > 0 {\n\t\t// Multiple type filter (from category checkboxes)\n\t\tph := make([]string, len(typesFilter))\n\t\tfor i := range ph {\n\t\t\tph[i] = \"?\"\n\t\t}\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"type IN (\" + strings.Join(ph, \", \") + \")\"\n\t\tfor _, t := range typesFilter {\n\t\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, t)\n\t\t}\n\t} else if isSimpleMode {\n\t\t// In simple mode with no explicit type filter, only show person-relevant event types\n\t\t// Build IN clause for simple mode types\n\t\tsimpleTypeList := make([]string, 0, len(simpleModeTypes))\n\t\tfor eventType := range simpleModeTypes {\n\t\t\tsimpleTypeList = append(simpleTypeList, eventType)\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Build placeholder string for IN clause\n\t\tplaceholders := make([]string, len(simpleTypeList))\n\t\tfor i := range placeholders {\n\t\t\tplaceholders[i] = \"?\"\n\t\t}\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"type IN (\" + strings.Join(placeholders, \", \") + \")\"\n\t\tfor _, eventType := range simpleTypeList {\n\t\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, eventType)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif zone != \"\" {\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"zone = ?\"\n\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, zone)\n\t}\n\tif person != \"\" {\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"person = ?\"\n\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, person)\n\t}\n\tif afterTS > 0 {\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"timestamp_ms >= ?\"\n\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, afterTS)\n\t}\n\tif untilTS > 0 {\n\t\t// Use < untilTS+1000 to include the entire second when the until timestamp\n\t\t// is in second precision (RFC3339 format truncates sub-seconds).\n\t\twhereSQL += \" AND \" + p + \"timestamp_ms < ?\"\n\t\twhereArgs = append(whereArgs, untilTS+1000)\n\t}\n\n\t// COUNT for total_filtered\n\tcountSQL := \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM \" + fromTable + \" WHERE \" + whereSQL\n\tvar totalFiltered int\n\tif err := e.db.QueryRow(countSQL, whereArgs...).Scan(&totalFiltered); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to count events\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Data query: add before cursor + ordering + limit\n\tdataWhere := whereSQL\n\tdataArgs := append([]interface{}{}, whereArgs...)\n\tif beforeTS > 0 {\n\t\tdataWhere += \" AND \" + p + \"timestamp_ms < ?\"\n\t\tdataArgs = append(dataArgs, beforeTS)\n\t}\n\t// untilTS is already included in the base WHERE clause via whereArgs\n\n\tdataSQL := \"SELECT \" + selectCols + \" FROM \" + fromTable +\n\t\t\" WHERE \" + dataWhere +\n\t\t\" ORDER BY \" + p + \"timestamp_ms DESC, \" + p + \"id DESC\" +\n\t\t\" LIMIT ?\"\n\tdataArgs = append(dataArgs, limit+1)\n\n\trows, err := e.db.Query(dataSQL, dataArgs...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to query events\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer rows.Close()\n\n\tevents := make([]*Event, 0, limit)\n\tfor rows.Next() {\n\t\tvar ev Event\n\t\tif err := rows.Scan(&ev.ID, &ev.Timestamp, &ev.Type, &ev.Zone,\n\t\t\t&ev.Person, &ev.BlobID, &ev.DetailJSON, &ev.Severity); err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tevents = append(events, &ev)\n\t}\n\n\thasMore := len(events) > limit\n\tif hasMore {\n\t\tevents = events[:limit]\n\t}\n\n\tcursor := \"\"\n\tif hasMore && len(events) > 0 {\n\t\tcursor = strconv.FormatInt(events[len(events)-1].Timestamp, 10)\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, eventsResponse{\n\t\tEvents:        events,\n\t\tCursor:        cursor,\n\t\tHasMore:       hasMore,\n\t\tTotalFiltered: totalFiltered,\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc (e *EventsHandler) getEvent(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tidStr := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\tid, err := strconv.ParseInt(idStr, 10, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid event id\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar event Event\n\terr = e.db.QueryRow(`\n\t\tSELECT id, timestamp_ms, type, zone, person, blob_id, detail_json, severity\n\t\tFROM events\n\t\tWHERE id = ?\n\t`, postEventFeedback(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tidStr := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\teventID, err := strconv.ParseInt(idStr, 10, 64)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, eventID).Scan(&exists)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, \"failed to query event\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif !exists {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusNotFound, req.Type != \"incorrect\" && req.Type != \"missed\" {\n\t\twriteJSONError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, \"invalid feedback type: must be 'correct', 'incorrect', or 'missed'\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Delegate to feedback handler if available\n\tif e.feedbackHandler != nil {\n\t\t// Use the feedback handler to process the request\n\t\ttype submitter interface {\n\t\t\tSubmitFeedback(w /api/replay/set-state.\n511\t// Changes the playback state for a replay session.\n512\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n513\t\tvar req setStateRequest\n514\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n515\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n516\t\t\treturn\n517\t\t}\n518\t\n519\t\tif req.State != \"playing\" && req.State != \"paused\" {\n520\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"state must handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n550\t// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\n551\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n552\t\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n553\t\n554\t\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n555\t\tif err != nil {\n556\t\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" session.FromMS\n574\t\tprogress := 0.0\n575\t\tif duration > 0 {\n576\t\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n580\t\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n581\t\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n582\t\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n583\t\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n584\t\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n585\t\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n586\t\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n587\t\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n588\t\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n589\t\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n590\t\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n591\t\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n592\t\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n593\t\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n594\t\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n595\t\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n596\t\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n597\t\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n598\t\t\t}\n599\t\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n603\t\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n604\t\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n605\t\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n606\t\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n607\t\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n608\t\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n609\t\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n610\t\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n611\t\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n612\t\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n613\t\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// sets it as the active session. Stops any previous active session first.\n630\tfunc (h *ReplayHandler) jumpToTime(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n631\t\tvar req jumpToTimeRequest\n632\t\tif err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, 1)\n666\t\tif err != nil {\n667\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": \"failed to start session: \" + (*ReplayHandler, error) {\n\t// Create replay worker\n\tworker := replay.NewWorker(store, nil, nil) // processor and broadcaster set later\n\n\treturn &ReplayHandler{\n\t\tworker:   worker,\n\t\tsessions: make(map[string]*_replaySession),\n\t\tnextID:    1,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// SetProcessorManager sets the signal processing pipeline for the replay worker.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetProcessorManager(pm interface{}) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\t// Type assertion to signal.ProcessorManager\n\tif procMgr, ok := pm.(*sigproc.ProcessorManager); ok {\n\t\th.worker.SetProcessorManager(procMgr)\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetBlobBroadcaster sets the blob broadcaster for replay results.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetBlobBroadcaster(broadcaster replay.BlobBroadcaster) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.worker.SetBroadcaster(broadcaster)\n}\n\n// SetFusionEngine sets the fusion engine for replay blob generation.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetFusionEngine(fusionEngine interface{}) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\t// Type assertion to fusion engine interface\n\tif engine, ok := fusionEngine.(interface {\n\t\tFuse(links []localization.LinkMotion) *localization.FusionResult\n\t\tSetNodePosition(mac string, x, z float64)\n\t}); ok {\n\t\th.worker.SetFusionEngine(engine)\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetSettingsHandler sets the settings handler for ApplyToLive functionality.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetSettingsHandler(settingsHandler SettingsPersister) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.settingsHandler = settingsHandler\n}\n\n// Start the replay worker.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Start() {\n\th.worker.Start()\n}\n\n// Stop the replay worker.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Stop() {\n\th.worker.Stop()\n}\n\n// Close closes the replay handler and the underlying store.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Close() error {\n\th.Stop()\n\tif h.worker != nil {\n\t\tif store := h.worker.GetStore(); store != nil {\n\t\t\treturn store.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// RegisterRoutes registers replay endpoints.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) RegisterRoutes(r chi.Router) {\n\tr.Get(\"/api/replay/sessions\", h.listSessions)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/start\", h.startSession)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/stop\", h.stopSession)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/seek\", h.seek)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/tune\", h.tune)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/set-speed\", h.setSpeed)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/set-state\", h.setState)\n\tr.Post(\"/api/replay/jump-to-time\", h.jumpToTime)\n\n\t// Session state endpoint for polling\n\tr.Get(\"/api/replay/session/{id}\", h.getSessionState)\n}\n\n// replayInfo represents the response from GET /api/replay/sessions.\ntype replayInfo struct {\n\tHasData   bool             `json:\"has_data\"`\n\tFileSize  int64            `json:\"file_size_mb\"`\n\tWritePos  int64            `json:\"write_pos\"`\n\tOldestPos int64            `json:\"oldest_pos\"`\n\tOldestTS  int64            `json:\"oldest_timestamp_ms\"`\n\tNewestTS  int64            `json:\"newest_timestamp_ms\"`\n\tSessions  []*_replaySession `json:\"sessions\"`\n}\n\n// listSessions handles GET /api/replay/sessions.\n// Returns replay store statistics and all active sessions.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) listSessions(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tsessions := make([]*_replaySession, 0, len(h.sessions))\n\tfor _, s := range h.sessions {\n\t\tsessions = append(sessions, s)\n\t}\n\th.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\t// Get oldest and newest timestamps\n\toldestTS := int64(0)\n\tnewestTS := int64(0)\n\tif stats.HasData {\n\t\t// Scan to find timestamps\n\t\th.scanTimestamps(&oldestTS, &newestTS)\n\t}\n\n\tinfo := replayInfo{\n\t\tHasData:   stats.HasData,\n\t\tFileSize:  stats.FileSize / (1024 * 1024),\n\t\tWritePos:  stats.WritePos,\n\t\tOldestPos: stats.OldestPos,\n\t\tOldestTS:  oldestTS,\n\t\tNewestTS:  newestTS,\n\t\tSessions:  sessions,\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, info)\n}\n\n// scanTimestamps scans the replay store to find oldest and newest timestamps.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) scanTimestamps(oldest, newest *int64) {\n\tstats := h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n\tif !stats.HasData {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Scan for oldest and newest\n\tstore := h.worker.GetStore()\n\tstore.Scan(func(recvTimeNS int64, frame []byte) bool {\n\t\trecvMS := recvTimeNS / 1e6\n\t\tif *oldest == 0 || recvMS < *oldest {\n\t\t\t*oldest = recvMS\n\t\t}\n\t\t*newest = recvMS\n\t\treturn true // continue to find newest\n\t})\n}\n\n// startSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/start.\ntype startSessionRequest struct {\n\t// FromISO8601 is the start timestamp in ISO8601 format (e.g., \"2024-03-15T14:30:00Z\")\n\tFromISO8601 string `json:\"from_iso8601\"`\n\t// ToISO8601 is the end timestamp in ISO8601 format. If empty, defaults to now.\n\tToISO8601 string `json:\"to_iso8601\"`\n\t// Speed is the playback speed multiplier: 1, 2, or 5. Defaults to 1.\n\tSpeed int `json:\"speed,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// startSession handles POST /api/replay/start.\n// Creates a new replay session for the specified time range.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) startSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req startSessionRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfromMS, err := parseISO8601(req.FromISO8601)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid from_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttoMS := timeNowMillis()\n\tif req.ToISO8601 != \"\" {\n\t\ttoMS, err = parseISO8601(req.ToISO8601)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid to_iso8601: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif toMS < fromMS {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"to_iso8601 must be after from_iso8601\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tspeed := req.Speed\n\tif speed == 0 {\n\t\tspeed = 1\n\t}\n\tif speed != 1 && speed != 2 && speed != 5 {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Start session via worker\n\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, speed)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Track session in API layer\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsession := &_replaySession{\n\t\tID:        sessionID,\n\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n\t\tCurrentMS: fromMS,\n\t\tSpeed:     speed,\n\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n\t}\n\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n\th.nextID++\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session started: %s (from %d to %d, speed %dx)\",\n\t\tsessionID, fromMS, toMS, speed)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n\t\t\"from_ms\":    fromMS,\n\t\t\"to_ms\":      toMS,\n\t\t\"speed\":      speed,\n\t\t\"state\":      \"paused\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// stopSessionRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/stop.\ntype stopSessionRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to stop.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n}\n\n// stopSession handles POST /api/replay/stop.\n// Stops the specified replay session and deletes it.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) stopSession(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req stopSessionRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(req.SessionID); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdelete(h.sessions, req.SessionID)\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session stopped: %s\", req.SessionID)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"stopped\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t})\n}\n\n// seekRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/seek.\ntype seekRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to seek within.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// TimestampISO8601 is the target timestamp in ISO8601 format.\n\tTimestampISO8601 string `json:\"timestamp_iso8601\"`\n}\n\n// seek handles POST /api/replay/seek.\n// Seeks to the specified timestamp within the session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) seek(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req seekRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\ttargetMS, err := parseISO8601(req.TimestampISO8601)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid timestamp: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(req.SessionID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tfromMS := session.FromMS\n\ttoMS := session.ToMS\n\n\tif targetMS < fromMS || targetMS > toMS {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.Seek(req.SessionID, targetMS); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"timestamp outside session range\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp outside session range\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.CurrentMS = targetMS\n\t\ts.State = \"paused\"\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session seeked: %s to %d\", req.SessionID, targetMS)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":      \"seeked\",\n\t\t\"current_ms\":  targetMS,\n\t\t\"frame_found\": true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// tuneRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/tune.\ntype tuneRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to tune.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// DeltaRMSThreshold is the motion detection threshold (0.001-1.0).\n\tDeltaRMSThreshold *float64 `json:\"delta_rms_threshold,omitempty\"`\n\t// TauS is the EMA baseline time constant in seconds (1-600).\n\tTauS *float64 `json:\"tau_s,omitempty\"`\n\t// FresnelDecay is the Fresnel zone weight decay rate (1.0-4.0).\n\tFresnelDecay *float64 `json:\"fresnel_decay,omitempty\"`\n\t// Subcarriers is the number of subcarriers for NBVI selection (8-47).\n\tSubcarriers *int `json:\"n_subcarriers,omitempty\"`\n\t// BreathingSensitivity is the breathing detection threshold in radians RMS (0.001-0.1).\n\tBreathingSensitivity *float64 `json:\"breathing_sensitivity,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// tune handles POST /api/replay/tune.\n// Updates pipeline parameters for the replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) tune(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req tuneRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif _, err := h.worker.GetSession(req.SessionID); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Build params map\n\tparams := make(map[string]interface{})\n\tif req.DeltaRMSThreshold != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"delta_rms_threshold\"] = *req.DeltaRMSThreshold\n\t}\n\tif req.TauS != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"tau_s\"] = *req.TauS\n\t}\n\tif req.FresnelDecay != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"fresnel_decay\"] = *req.FresnelDecay\n\t}\n\tif req.Subcarriers != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"n_subcarriers\"] = *req.Subcarriers\n\t}\n\tif req.BreathingSensitivity != nil {\n\t\tparams[\"breathing_sensitivity\"] = *req.BreathingSensitivity\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.UpdateParams(req.SessionID, params); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session tuned: %s params=%+v\", req.SessionID, params)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"tuned\",\n\t\t\"params\":  params,\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t})\n}\n\n// setSpeedRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-speed.\ntype setSpeedRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to modify.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// Speed is the playback speed multiplier: 1, 2, or 5.\n\tSpeed int `json:\"speed\"`\n}\n\n// setSpeed handles POST /api/replay/set-speed.\n// Changes the playback speed for a replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setSpeed(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req setSpeedRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.Speed != 1 && req.Speed != 2 && req.Speed != 5 {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"speed must be 1, 2, or 5\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.SetPlaybackSpeed(req.SessionID, req.Speed); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.Speed = req.Speed\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session speed changed: %s to %dx\", req.SessionID, req.Speed)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"speed_set\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t\t\"speed\":   req.Speed,\n\t})\n}\n\n// setStateRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/set-state.\ntype setStateRequest struct {\n\t// SessionID is the ID of the session to modify.\n\tSessionID string `json:\"session_id\"`\n\t// State is the playback state: \"playing\" or \"paused\".\n\tState string `json:\"state\"`\n}\n\n// setState handles POST /api/replay/set-state.\n// Changes the playback state for a replay session.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) setState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req setStateRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.State != \"playing\" && req.State != \"paused\" {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"state must be 'playing' or 'paused'\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif err := h.worker.SetState(req.SessionID, req.State); err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[req.SessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.State = req.State\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Replay session state changed: %s to %s\", req.SessionID, req.State)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"status\":  \"state_set\",\n\t\t\"session\": req.SessionID,\n\t\t\"state\":   req.State,\n\t})\n}\n\n// getSessionState handles GET /api/replay/session/{id}.\n// Returns the current state of a replay session including blobs.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) getSessionState(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tsessionID := chi.URLParam(r, \"id\")\n\n\tsession, err := h.worker.GetSession(sessionID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif err.Error() == \"session not found\" {\n\t\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{\"error\": \"session not found\"})\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update API layer session state\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tif s, exists := h.sessions[sessionID]; exists {\n\t\ts.CurrentMS = session.CurrentMS\n\t\ts.State = session.State\n\t}\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Calculate progress\n\tduration := session.ToMS - session.FromMS\n\tprogress := 0.0\n\tif duration > 0 {\n\t\tprogress = float64(session.CurrentMS-session.FromMS) / float64(duration)\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert replay blobs to API format\n\tblobs := make([]map[string]interface{}, 0, len(session.LastBlobs))\n\tfor _, b := range session.LastBlobs {\n\t\tblob := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"id\":                  b.ID,\n\t\t\t\"x\":                   b.X,\n\t\t\t\"y\":                   b.Y,\n\t\t\t\"z\":                   b.Z,\n\t\t\t\"vx\":                  b.VX,\n\t\t\t\"vy\":                  b.VY,\n\t\t\t\"vz\":                  b.VZ,\n\t\t\t\"weight\":              b.Weight,\n\t\t\t\"posture\":             b.Posture,\n\t\t\t\"person_id\":           b.PersonID,\n\t\t\t\"person_label\":        b.PersonLabel,\n\t\t\t\"person_color\":        b.PersonColor,\n\t\t\t\"identity_confidence\": b.IdentityConfidence,\n\t\t\t\"identity_source\":     b.IdentitySource,\n\t\t\t\"trail\":               b.Trail,\n\t\t}\n\t\tblobs = append(blobs, blob)\n\t}\n\n\t// Build response with session state and blobs\n\tresponse := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"session_id\":  sessionID,\n\t\t\"current_ms\":  session.CurrentMS,\n\t\t\"from_ms\":     session.FromMS,\n\t\t\"to_ms\":       session.ToMS,\n\t\t\"state\":       session.State,\n\t\t\"speed\":       session.Speed,\n\t\t\"progress\":    progress,\n\t\t\"params\":      session.Params,\n\t\t\"blobs\":       blobs,\n\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": session.LastBlobTime,\n\t}\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, response)\n}\n\n// jumpToTimeRequest represents the request body for POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\ntype jumpToTimeRequest struct {\n\t// TimestampMS is the target timestamp in epoch milliseconds.\n\tTimestampMS int64 `json:\"timestamp_ms\"`\n\t// WindowMS is the time window around the timestamp (default 5000ms).\n\tWindowMS int64 `json:\"window_ms,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// jumpToTime handles POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\n// Creates a replay session centered on the given timestamp, seeks to it,\n// and sets it as the active session. Stops any previous active session first.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) jumpToTime(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tvar req jumpToTimeRequest\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"invalid request body: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif req.TimestampMS <= 0 {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{\"error\": \"timestamp_ms is required and must be positive\"})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\twindowMS := req.WindowMS\n\tif windowMS <= 0 {\n\t\twindowMS = 5000 // ±5 seconds default\n\t}\n\n\tfromMS := req.TimestampMS - windowMS\n\ttoMS := req.TimestampMS + windowMS\n\n\t// Stop previous active session if any\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tprevID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif prevID != \"\" {\n\t\tif err := h.worker.StopSession(prevID); err == nil {\n\t\t\th.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\tdelete(h.sessions, prevID)\n\t\t\th.mu.Unlock()\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Previous replay session stopped: %s\", prevID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Start new session\n\tsessionID, err := h.worker.StartSession(fromMS, toMS, 1)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{\"error\": \"failed to start session: \" + err.Error()})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Seek to target timestamp\n\tif err := h.worker.Seek(sessionID, req.TimestampMS); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] Jump-to-time seek failed: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Track in API layer\n\tsession := &_replaySession{\n\t\tID:        sessionID,\n\t\tFromMS:    fromMS,\n\t\tToMS:      toMS,\n\t\tCurrentMS: req.TimestampMS,\n\t\tSpeed:     1,\n\t\tState:     \"paused\",\n\t\tParams:    make(map[string]interface{}),\n\t\tCreatedAt: formatTimestamp(fromMS),\n\t}\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\th.sessions[sessionID] = session\n\th.activeSessionID = sessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[INFO] Jump-to-time: session %s at %d (window ±%dms)\", sessionID, req.TimestampMS, windowMS)\n\n\twriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"session_id\":   sessionID,\n\t\t\"timestamp_ms\": req.TimestampMS,\n\t\t\"from_ms\":      fromMS,\n\t\t\"to_ms\":        toMS,\n\t\t\"state\":        \"paused\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// Helper functions\nfunc timeNowMillis() int64 {\n\treturn time.Now().UnixNano() / 1e6\n}\n\nfunc parseISO8601(s string) (int64, error) {\n\tt, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, s)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\treturn t.UnixNano() / 1e6, nil\n}\n\nfunc formatTimestamp(ms int64) string {\n\treturn time.Unix(ms/1000, (ms%1000)*1e6).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n}\n\n// GetReplayPath returns the path to the CSI replay binary file.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetReplayPath() string {\n\tif h.replayPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn h.replayPath\n\t}\n\treturn \"/data/csi_replay.bin\" // Default path\n}\n\n// SetReplayPath sets the path to the CSI replay binary file.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetReplayPath(path string) {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer h.mu.Unlock()\n\th.replayPath = path\n}\n\n// GetStoreStats returns statistics about the replay store.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetStoreStats() replay.StoreStats {\n\treturn h.worker.GetStoreStats()\n}\n\n// GetSessions returns a list of all active replay sessions.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) GetSessions() []SessionInfo {\n\th.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer h.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tsessions := make([]SessionInfo, 0, len(h.sessions))\n\tfor _, s := range h.sessions {\n\t\tsessions = append(sessions, SessionInfo{\n\t\t\tID:        s.ID,\n\t\t\tFromMS:    s.FromMS,\n\t\t\tToMS:      s.ToMS,\n\t\t\tCurrentMS: s.CurrentMS,\n\t\t\tState:     s.State,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn sessions\n}\n\n// SeekTo moves the active replay session to the target timestamp.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SeekTo(targetMS int64) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn h.worker.Seek(sessionID, targetMS)\n}\n\n// Play starts playback of the active replay session at the specified speed.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Play(speed float64) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert float speed to int (1x=1, 2x=2, 0.5x=1 for now)\n\tspeedInt := 1\n\tif speed >= 2.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = 2\n\t} else if speed >= 5.0 {\n\t\tspeedInt = 5\n\t}\n\n\t// Set speed first\n\tif err := h.worker.SetPlaybackSpeed(sessionID, speedInt); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Then set state to playing\n\treturn h.worker.SetState(sessionID, \"playing\")\n}\n\n// Pause pauses playback of the active replay session.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) Pause() error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no active replay session\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn h.worker.SetState(sessionID, \"paused\")\n}\n\n// SetParams updates the replay pipeline parameters for the active session.\n// Implements dashboard.ReplayHandler interface.\nfunc (h *ReplayHandler) SetParams(params *replay.TunableParams) error {\n\th.mu.Lock()\n\tsessionID := h.activeSessionID\n\th.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif sessionID == \"\" the full lifecycle: start -> tune -> seek -> stop.\n808\tfunc TestReplaySessionLifecycle(t *testing.T) {\n809\t\thandler := newTestReplayHandler(t, true)\n810\t\tr := setupReplayRouter(handler)\n811\t\n812\t\tpastTime := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n813\t\tmidTime := time.Now().Add(-30 * time.Minute).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n814\t\n815\t\t// 1. Start a session\n816\t\tstartBody, _ := json.Marshal(startSessionRequest{\n817\t\t\tFromISO8601: pastTime,\n818\t\t\tSpeed:       2,\n819\t\t})\n820\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/start\", bytes.NewReader(startBody))\n821\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n822\t\trr := httptest.NewRecorder()\n823\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n824\t\n825\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n826\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Start: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n827\t\t}\n828\t\n829\t\tvar startResp map[string]interface{}\n830\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&startResp); err != nil {\n831\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode start response: %v\", err)\n832\t\t}\n833\t\n834\t\tsessionID, ok := startResp[\"session_id\"].(string)\n835\t\tif !ok || sessionID == \"\" {\n836\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-empty session_id\")\n837\t\t}\n838\t\n839\t\t// 2. Tune the session\n840\t\tthreshold := 0.03\n841\t\ttuneBody, _ := json.Marshal(tuneRequest{\n842\t\t\tSessionID:        sessionID,\n843\t\t\tDeltaRMSThreshold: &threshold,\n844\t\t})\n845\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/tune\", bytes.NewReader(tuneBody))\n846\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n847\t\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n848\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n849\t\n850\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n851\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Tune: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n852\t\t}\n853\t\n854\t\t// 3. Seek within the session\n855\t\tseekBody, _ := json.Marshal(seekRequest{\n856\t\t\tSessionID:        sessionID,\n857\t\t\tTimestampISO8601: midTime,\n858\t\t})\n859\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/seek\", bytes.NewReader(seekBody))\n860\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n861\t\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n862\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n863\t\n864\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n865\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Seek: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n866\t\t}\n867\t\n868\t\t// 4. Verify session appears in list\n869\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n870\t\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n871\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n872\t\n873\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n874\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"List: expected 200, got %d\", rr.Code)\n875\t\t}\n876\t\n877\t\tvar listResp map[string]interface{}\n878\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp); err != nil {\n879\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode list response: %v\", err)\n880\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\tsessions, ok := listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n883\t\tif !ok {\n884\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected sessions array, got %T\", listResp[\"sessions\"])\n885\t\t}\n886\t\tif len(sessions) != 1 {\n887\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 session, got %d\", len(sessions))\n888\t\t}\n889\t\n890\t\t// 5. Stop the session\n891\t\tstopBody, _ := json.Marshal(stopSessionRequest{\n892\t\t\tSessionID: sessionID,\n893\t\t})\n894\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/stop\", bytes.NewReader(stopBody))\n895\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n896\t\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n897\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n898\t\n899\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n900\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Stop: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n901\t\t}\n902\t\n903\t\t// 6. Verify session is removed from list\n904\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n905\t\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n906\t\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n907\t\n908\t\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n909\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"List after stop: expected 200, got %d\", rr.Code)\n910\t\t}\n911\t\n912\t\tjson.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp)\n913\t\tsessions, _ = listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n914\t\tif len(sessions) != 0 {\n915\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 sessions after stop, got %d\", len(sessions))\n916\t\t}\n917\t}\n918\t\n919\t// TestMultipleSessions tests managing multiple concurrent replay sessions.\n920\tfunc TestMultipleSessions(t *testing.T) {\n921\t\thandler := newTestReplayHandler(t, true)\n922\t\tr := TestReplaySessionLifecycle(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := newTestReplayHandler(t, true)\n\tr := setupReplayRouter(handler)\n\n\tpastTime := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tmidTime := time.Now().Add(-30 * time.Minute).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\n\t// 1. Start a session\n\tstartBody, _ := json.Marshal(startSessionRequest{\n\t\tFromISO8601: pastTime,\n\t\tSpeed:       2,\n\t})\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/start\", bytes.NewReader(startBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Start: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n\t}\n\n\tvar startResp map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&startResp); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode start response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tsessionID, ok := startResp[\"session_id\"].(string)\n\tif !ok || sessionID == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Expected non-empty session_id\")\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Tune the session\n\tthreshold := 0.03\n\ttuneBody, _ := json.Marshal(tuneRequest{\n\t\tSessionID:        sessionID,\n\t\tDeltaRMSThreshold: &threshold,\n\t})\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/tune\", bytes.NewReader(tuneBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Tune: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. Seek within the session\n\tseekBody, _ := json.Marshal(seekRequest{\n\t\tSessionID:        sessionID,\n\t\tTimestampISO8601: midTime,\n\t})\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/seek\", bytes.NewReader(seekBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Seek: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Verify session appears in list\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"List: expected 200, got %d\", rr.Code)\n\t}\n\n\tvar listResp map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to decode list response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tsessions, ok := listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected sessions array, got %T\", listResp[\"sessions\"])\n\t}\n\tif len(sessions) != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 session, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n\n\t// 5. Stop the session\n\tstopBody, _ := json.Marshal(stopSessionRequest{\n\t\tSessionID: sessionID,\n\t})\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/stop\", bytes.NewReader(stopBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Stop: expected 200, got %d: %s\", rr.Code, rr.Body.String())\n\t}\n\n\t// 6. Verify session is removed from list\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tif rr.Code != http.StatusOK {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"List after stop: expected 200, got %d\", rr.Code)\n\t}\n\n\tjson.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp)\n\tsessions, _ = listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n\tif len(sessions) != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 sessions after stop, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestMultipleSessions tests managing multiple concurrent replay sessions.\nfunc TestMultipleSessions(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := newTestReplayHandler(t, true)\n\tr := setupReplayRouter(handler)\n\n\tpastTime1 := time.Now().Add(-2 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\tpastTime2 := time.Now().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format(time.RFC3339Nano)\n\n\t// Start two sessions\n\tstartBody, _ := json.Marshal(startSessionRequest{\n\t\tFromISO8601: pastTime1,\n\t\tSpeed:       1,\n\t})\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/start\", bytes.NewReader(startBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, bytes.NewReader(startBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tvar resp2 \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tvar listResp map[string]interface{}\n\tjson.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp)\n\tsessions, _ := listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n\n\tif len(sessions) != 2 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Expected 2 sessions, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n\n\t// Stop first session\n\tstopBody, _ := json.Marshal(stopSessionRequest{\n\t\tSessionID: sessionID1,\n\t})\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/stop\", bytes.NewReader(stopBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\t// Verify one session remains\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tjson.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp)\n\tsessions, _ = listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n\n\tif len(sessions) != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 session after stopping one, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n\n\t// Stop second session\n\tstopBody, _ = json.Marshal(stopSessionRequest{\n\t\tSessionID: sessionID2,\n\t})\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/replay/stop\", bytes.NewReader(stopBody))\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\t// Verify no sessions remain\n\treq = httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"/api/replay/sessions\", nil)\n\trr = httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tr.ServeHTTP(rr, req)\n\n\tjson.NewDecoder(rr.Body).Decode(&listResp)\n\tsessions, _ = listResp[\"sessions\"].([]interface{})\n\n\tif len(sessions) != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 sessions after stopping both, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestGetSessions tests the GetSessions method.\nfunc TestGetSessions(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := newTestReplayHandler(t, true)\n\n\t// Initially empty\n\tsessions := handler.GetSessions()\n\tif len(sessions) != 0 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 0 sessions initially, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n\n\t// Create a session\n\thandler.mu.Lock()\n\thandler.sessions[\"test-session\"] = &_replaySession{\n\t\tID:     \"test-session\",\n\t\tFromMS: 1000,\n\t\tToMS:   2000,\n\t\tState:  \"paused\",\n\t}\n\thandler.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tsessions = handler.GetSessions()\n\tif len(sessions) != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected 1 session, got %d\", len(sessions))\n\t}\n\tif sessions[0].ID != \"test-session\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected session ID 'test-session', got %s\", sessions[0].ID)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestGetReplayPath tests the GetReplayPath method.\nfunc TestGetReplayPath(t *testing.T) path)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestClose tests the Close method.\nfunc TestClose(t *testing.T) {\n\tstore := &mockRecordingStore{}\n\thandler, err := NewReplayHandler(store)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewReplayHandler: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := handler.Close(); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Close returned error: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !store.closed {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected store to be closed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestCloseWithError tests Close when store returns an error.\nfunc TestCloseWithError(t *testing.T) {\n\texpectedErr := fmt.Errorf(\"close failed\")\n\tstore := &mockRecordingStore{closeErr: expectedErr}\n\thandler, err := NewReplayHandler(store)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"NewReplayHandler: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif err := handler.Close(); err != expectedErr {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error %v, got %v\", expectedErr, err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestParseISO8601 tests the parseISO8601 helper function.\nfunc TestParseISO8601(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname    string\n\t\tinput   string\n\t\twantErr bool\n\t\tcheck   func(int64) bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"valid RFC3339 timestamp\",\n\t\t\tinput:   \"2024-03-15T14:30:00Z\",\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tcheck: func(ms int64) bool {\n\t\t\t\texpected := int64(1710513000000) // 2024-03-15 14:30:00 UTC in ms\n\t\t\t\treturn ms == expected\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"valid RFC3339Nano timestamp\",\n\t\t\tinput:   \"2024-03-15T14:30:00.123456789Z\",\n\t\t\twantErr: false,\n\t\t\tcheck: func(ms int64) bool {\n\t\t\t\treturn ms > 1710513000000 && ms < 1710513000200\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"invalid timestamp\",\n\t\t\tinput:   \"not-a-timestamp\",\n\t\t\twantErr: true,\n\t\t\tcheck:   nil,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:    \"empty string\",\n\t\t\tinput:   \"\",\n\t\t\twantErr: true,\n\t\t\tcheck:   nil,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tms, err := parseISO8601(tt.input)\n\t\t\tif (err != nil) != tt.wantErr {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"parseISO8601(%q) error = %v, wantErr %v\", tt.input, err, tt.wantErr)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif !tt.wantErr && tt.check != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif !tt.check(ms) {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"parseISO8601(%q) = %d, check failed\", tt.input, ms)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatTimestamp tests the formatTimestamp helper function.\nfunc TestFormatTimestamp(t *testing.T) {\n\tms := int64(1710519800000) // 2024-03-15 14:30:00 UTC\n\tformatted := formatTimestamp(ms)\n\n\tif formatted == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"formatTimestamp returned empty string\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify it can be parsed back\n\t_, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, formatted)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"formatTimestamp(%d) returned invalid format: %v\", ms, err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestJumpToTime tests POST /api/replay/jump-to-time.\nfunc TestJumpToTime(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname       string\n\t\tbody       interface{}\n\t\twantStatus int\n\t\tsetup      func(*ReplayHandler)\n\t\tcheck      func(*testing.T, *ReplayHandler, map[string]interface{})\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"jump with valid timestamp\",\n\t\t\tbody: jumpToTimeRequest{\n\t\t\t\tTimestampMS: 1710519800000,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\twantStatus: http.StatusOK,\n\t\t\tcheck: func(t *testing.T, h *ReplayHandler, resp map[string]interface{}) {\n\t\t\t\tif _, ok := resp[\"session_id\"].(string); !ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected session_id string, got %v\", resp[\"session_id\"])\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif ts, ok := resp[\"timestamp_ms\"].(float64); !ok || int64(ts) != 1710519800000 {\n\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected timestamp_ms=1710519800000, got %v\", resp[\"timestamp_ms\"])\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif from, ok := resp[\"from_ms\"].(float64); !ok ||
